validation_rules: require a Mathlib import probe status before any Mathlib availability claim
reject Mathlib-dependent consumer cases that skip the readiness gate
treat translation smoke rows as non-proof metadata
mark absent corpora as blocked capabilities
reject proof, provider, or oracle body fields
reject private source refs and release/proof-authority overclaims
emit private-state scan and redacted receipts
receipt_expectations: receipt must cite source pattern ids, source refs, corpus counts, blocked capabilities, consumer-gate decisions, negative-case coverage, private-state scan, authority ceiling, and anti-claim
anti_claim: This standard governs public corpus/toolchain readiness metadata only. It does not run Lean/Lake, claim Mathlib availability, prove theorem correctness, benchmark formal-math corpora, expose proof bodies, call providers, or authorize release.

standard_payload.ontology: A public-safe formal-math corpus readiness gate that makes Mathlib absence explicit before downstream retrieval, tactic routing, or proof-witness work.
standard_payload.teleology: Let Microcosm show why formal-math consumers must gate Mathlib-dependent work instead of pretending a public clone has the private lab's corpus environment.
standard_payload.anti_purpose: Turning a readiness fixture into proof authority, corpus benchmark authority, or release authority.
failure_modes: Mathlib availability is claimed without a passing probe
translation smoke rows imply proof authority
absent corpus is treated as usable
documentation claims release or proof authority

omission_receipt.reason: public tranche carries fixture-level metadata, source-ref labels, negative cases, and redacted receipts only

anti_claim_contract.rule: Corpus readiness material must state that it is not Lean/Lake execution, Mathlib proof authority, benchmark authority, provider authority, or release authority

source_coupling_contract.rule: macro source refs stay path-level metadata and public replacement refs stay public-root relative
